author.name: Daniel Hudon
author.major: IMGD BA - Technical Art Concentration - Computer Science Minor
author.instructions: Fill out all relevant fields in this document. If you are not using a field (i.e., you don't have any code blocks) then fully delete that field. Within the 'projects' array below, include 1 entry for each of your experiments. You can copy and past for additional projects.

projects.description: A post-processing shader based on datamoshing techniques that uses motion vectors to create a distortion effect to use for 'glitches' or cloaking. Contains parameters to control via material instance: Intensity, NoiseScale, StencilMaskValue, VelocityScale; and toggles for UseNoise, UseStencilMask, and VisibleThroughObjects. Also included is a particle effect I used with the shader.
A slime effect consisting of a metaball shader and niagara particles. Takes the form of a slime ball that sticks to nearby surfaces, and bubbles that stick to the main body.

Two types of rain particle systems in niagara. The CPU Rain spawns splashes on impact but has a higher performance cost, while the GPU Rain simply has the drops bounce and has a lower performance cost.
A puff of smoke Niagara particle effect for use as a morph animation. Also included is the blueprint code to trigger the morph.
